definition: A warlock is, in modern fantasy, a male magic-user — often one who draws power from a pact with a darker patron. By origin the word has nothing to do with being male: it means an oath-breaker.
history: Old English *wǣrloga* is 'oath-breaker, traitor' — *wǣr*, 'pledge,' + *-loga*, 'liar.' Applied first to the Devil himself, the great covenant-breaker, it drifted in Scots toward 'a man in league with the Devil,' and thence to the fantasy 'male witch.' The pact-with-a-patron flavor of the modern warlock is, remarkably, latent in the etymology: a warlock is defined by a broken or dark covenant.

etymology.first: ‘traitor’ in OE; ‘male witch / one in league with the Devil’ by the 1500s (esp. Scots)
etymology.note: Not a male counterpart to ‘witch’ by origin at all — a warlock is an oath-breaker, a covenant-traitor.
etymology.hook: The broken-oath etymology behind every fantasy warlock.

essay.title: Warlock: the broken oath
essay.dek: Not a male witch by origin — an oath-breaker. The pact is in the word.
essay.html:
Modern fantasy treats a warlock as a male magic-user, often one who draws power from a bargain with a darker patron — the pact-caster of Dungeons & Dragons and of Magic: The Gathering. By origin the word has nothing to do with being male, and everything to do with the bargain.

Old English wǣrloga means 'oath-breaker, traitor' — wǣr, 'a pledge, a covenant,' plus -loga, 'a liar,' from lēogan, 'to lie.' A warlock is, at the root, a covenant-breaker, and the word's first great referent was the Devil himself: the archetypal traitor, the being who broke faith with God. Only later, and chiefly in Scots, did it drift toward 'a man in league with the Devil,' and thence to the fantasy 'male witch' — a folk-symmetry with witch that the etymology never actually supported.

What is uncanny is how the modern game-warlock circles back to the oath. The Dungeons & Dragons warlock is defined by a pact sworn to a patron — a fiend, a fey, a Great Old One — and the whole drama of the class is that the power is borrowed on terms. That is, precisely, a covenant, and the danger in it is precisely that covenants can be broken. A thousand years on, the warlock is still defined by an oath: not the pledge kept, like the paladin's, but the pledge made in the dark, and never quite safe.

cards.name: Exdeath, Void Warlock // Neo Exdeath, Dimension's End
cards.type_line: Legendary Creature — Spirit Warlock // Legendary Creature — Spirit Avatar

flavor_snippets.html: The Night Serpent tasted the <mark>warlock</mark>'s ambition and savored the dark hunger in her soul.

"See how the statues all have chunks bitten out of them? We're dealing with a basilisk, or I'm a froghemoth."
—Larissa Kyriln, learned <mark>warlock</mark>

"What's softer than silk, stronger than steel, and quieter than thought?"
—<mark>Warlock</mark> riddle
Unlike most <mark>warlocks</mark>, those who make pacts with archfey are drawn to pranks and whimsy moreso than death and darkness.
Burning slowly into annihilation, the elder evil Hadar grants fearsome powers to <mark>warlocks</mark> willing to help satiate its dark hunger.

mechanics_snippets.html: Enchanted land has "{T}: Add two mana of any one color. Spend this mana only to cast outlaw spells or activate abilities of outlaw sources." (Assassins, Mercenaries, Pirates, Rogues, and <mark>Warlocks</mark> are outlaws.)
Other outlaws you control have vigilance and haste. (Assassins, Mercenaries, Pirates, Rogues, and <mark>Warlocks</mark> are outlaws.)
Other outlaws you control have haste. (Assassins, Mercenaries, Pirates, Rogues, and <mark>Warlocks</mark> are outlaws.)

When you cast this spell, copy it if you control a planeswalker, Cleric, Druid, Shaman, <mark>Warlock</mark>, or Wizard.
Scry 1, then draw a card.
Destroy target non-outlaw creature. (Assassins, Mercenaries, Pirates, Rogues, and <mark>Warlocks</mark> are outlaws. Everyone else is fair game.)

Target creature gets -1/-0 until end of turn. It gets -4/-0 until end of turn instead if you control an outlaw. (Assassins, Mercenaries, Pirates, Rogues, and <mark>Warlocks</mark> are outlaws.)
Draw a card.
